LABOR FIGHT

REAFFiRMS ALLEGIANCE TO
A. =. OF L. AND PUT LABOR
RECORDS OF ALL CANDI-


DATES BEFORE THE PUBLIC.

pages.raw_text:
Resolved, that the Washington
State Federation of Labor re-
affirms its allegiance to the parent
organization, the American Fedora
ton of Labor, both industrially and
politically, pledging our influence
to stand faithfully by our friends,
oppose and defeat our enemies,
whether they be candidates for
president, for. congress or other
offices, whether executive, legisla-
tive or judicial" We further af-
firm that we recognize that it is
Just as much our duty to conform
to conform to the political pro-
gram as outlined by the American
Federation of Labor as it is our
duty to conform to any other prin-
ciples promulgated by the parent
body.


This resolution will be printed as
the heading of the answers to the
pledges from those candidates as
piring to state Offices, and will be
sent to all the union men through
out the different counties, to the
state grange and to places where
/ they have no unions.


The answers to the pledge blanks
! that have been sent to these can
didates will be printed in full, and
no attention will De paid to those
who answered by letters or other
wise except filling out the regular
form.


E. A. Cooney, second vice presi-
dent of the executive and legisla-
, tive committee of the State FEEDER
alton of Labor, returned this morn.
ing from North Yakima, where the
committee was in session yester-
: day. There were nine members in
: attendance The state has heen
